skills.name: Publish ACP commerce discovery
skills.description: The Agentic Commerce Protocol lets agents discover your commerce API and create checkout sessions without bespoke integration.

skills.name: Publish an agent skills index
skills.description: A skills index lets agents discover machine-readable instructions for working with your site — what they can do and how, with integrity hashes.

skills.name: Declare AI crawler rules in robots.txt
skills.description: AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini) crawl with named user-agents. Explicit rules let you control whether they may read and cite your content — silence means inconsistent behavior across providers.

skills.name: Structure content as answer units
skills.description: Question-style headings followed by concise, direct answers are the format AI engines quote most. Walls of prose are harder to extract and cite.

skills.name: Publish an API catalog (RFC 9727)
skills.description: An API catalog lets agents find your service description (OpenAPI), documentation, and status endpoints programmatically — the entry point for automated integration.

skills.name: Declare an absolute canonical URL
skills.description: Canonical URLs consolidate duplicate/variant URLs so engines index and cite the single correct version — avoiding split signals and wrong citations.

skills.name: Declare AI usage with Content Signals
skills.description: Crawl permission and usage permission are different things. Content Signals let you state, per purpose, whether your content may be used for AI training, classic search, or AI answer input — independent of whether a bot may crawl.

skills.name: Mark up FAQs with FAQPage schema
skills.description: FAQPage structured data is a strong, directly-citable answer-unit format — AI engines quote question/answer pairs verbatim.

skills.name: Use exactly one clear H1
skills.description: A single descriptive <h1> tells crawlers and answer engines the page's primary topic. Zero or multiple H1s create ambiguity about what the page is about.

skills.name: Add JSON-LD structured data
skills.description: JSON-LD lets answer engines understand entities (organization, product, article, FAQ) precisely, increasing the odds of accurate citation and rich presentation.

skills.name: Add Link response headers (RFC 8288)
skills.description: Link response headers let agents discover related resources (API catalog, docs, manifests) without parsing HTML — fast, language-agnostic discovery.

skills.name: Serve Markdown to agents on request
skills.description: Agents parse Markdown far more reliably than HTML, with fewer tokens and fewer extraction errors. Content-negotiating Markdown gives agents a clean read while browsers still get HTML.

skills.name: Publish an MCP Server Card
skills.description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server Card advertises your MCP server so agents can discover and connect to your tools without manual configuration.

skills.name: Provide title and meta description
skills.description: <title> and <meta name=description> are used directly in search results and AI answer snippets. Missing or poorly sized values lead to truncated or auto-generated snippets you don't control.

skills.name: Support MPP machine payments
skills.description: The Machine Payment Protocol advertises payable operations in your OpenAPI so agents can discover prices and transact programmatically.

skills.name: Publish OAuth/OIDC discovery metadata
skills.description: Discovery metadata lets agents learn programmatically how to authenticate with your protected APIs — endpoints, supported grants, and key material — without hardcoding.

skills.name: Publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata
skills.description: RFC 9728 metadata tells agents which authorization servers can issue tokens for your resource and which scopes exist — so they can obtain the right access token.

skills.name: Add Open Graph tags
skills.description: Open Graph metadata controls how pages appear when shared and gives agents a reliable title/image/type to extract.

skills.name: Publish a valid robots.txt
skills.description: robots.txt is the first file crawlers and AI agents read to learn how they may access your site. A missing or malformed file leaves agents guessing.

skills.name: Publish and reference a sitemap.xml
skills.description: A sitemap tells crawlers and agents which URLs are canonical and worth indexing, with freshness signals. It improves coverage and how quickly new content is discovered.

skills.name: Enable Universal Commerce Protocol
skills.description: UCP lets agents discover your commerce capabilities, services, and endpoints from a single well-known profile.

skills.name: Set viewport and language
skills.description: A viewport meta signals mobile-friendliness; <html lang> declares content language. Both help crawlers, agents, and accessibility tools interpret your page correctly.

skills.name: Sign bot requests with Web Bot Auth
skills.description: If your service makes outbound bot/agent requests, publishing a signing-key directory lets receiving sites verify your requests via HTTP Message Signatures — building trust and avoiding blocks.

skills.name: Expose tools via WebMCP
skills.description: WebMCP lets in-browser AI agents call your site's key actions as typed tools — turning a page into an agent-operable surface.

skills.name: Support x402 agent payments
skills.description: x402 lets AI agents pay for access to protected routes automatically over HTTP 402 — enabling pay-per-use APIs and content for autonomous agents.
